name: chr3L_telomere
description: Drosophila telomere — fundamentally different from vertebrate telomeres. Drosophila has lost telomerase and uses three non-LTR retrotransposons (HeT-A, TART, TAHRE) that target chromosome ends. HeT-A is the most abundant (~80% of telomere mass), TART ~15%, TAHRE rare. Repeats are head-to-tail polymers ~6-10 kb each, telomeres range 5-30 kb. Adjacent TAS (telomere-associated sequence) extends another 10-30 kb of complex repeat.
expected_copy_count: 1-10 HeT-A/TART/TAHRE copies per chromosome end, variable
